stimulus.content: In the 1840s, a disease destroyed most of the potato crop in Ireland. Potatoes were the main food source for millions of Irish people. As a result, over one million people left Ireland and moved to the United States and other countries.

stem: What type of migration factor caused so many Irish people to leave their country?

choices.text: A push factor caused by a food shortage
A pull factor from better jobs in Ireland
A push factor caused by overcrowded cities
A pull factor from warmer weather elsewhere

answer_explanation: The Irish Potato Famine was a push factor, a negative condition that forced people to leave. The food shortage pushed over one million people to migrate to other countries.
